ClinicalDocument.title: Exit from Mental Health and Substance Misuse Specialist Services

code.displayName: Mental Health Triage Decision Identifies Need as Urgent
Proxy Care Coordinator/Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker
Care Process Date Commenced or First Review
Care Process Date Concluded or Most Recent Review

section.title: Aliases or Other Names
section.text: Tony often uses a nickname 'Maca'.

section.title: Communication and Comprehension
section.text: Tony needs his hearing aid to hear clearly - a visit from an Audiologist has provided Tony with a new rechargeable hearing aid - although he does still need support to ensure he keeps it recharged and cleaned. Native Language Irish.

section.title: Housing
section.text: Tony has been found a bedsit flatin the Uptown Housing Association development on Smith Street. Living on own. Private rented.

section.title: Property Access Information
section.text: You will need to use the intercom to gain access.

section.title: Ethnicity, Religion, Philosophy & Culture
section.text:
Tony has been attending the Church of the Virgin Mary, Uptown some Sunday mornings.
Ethnicity: White Irish.
Religion Philosophy: Roman Catholic Church.

section.title: Education, Training, Employment and Financial Status
section.text: A supervisory role has been found for Tony within a sheltered work environment.

section.title: Financial Status
section.text: Tony is currently in receipt of Incapacity Benefit since 02/04/2004, which maybe withdrawn once he commences the employment found for him.

section.title: Referral, First Contact, Triage and Entry
Referral / First Contact
section.text: Referral Date: 01/01/2004

section.title: Reason for Referral / First Contact
section.text: Tony was expressing some grandiose ideas about who he is that lead staff at the hostel to become concerned for him. He can be aggressive and personally invasive, however he had not been observed being physically violent.

section.title: Triage Decision
section.text: Date Recorded: 01/01/2004. URGENT. The referral indicates that it is important that Tony is seen urgently.

section.title: Specialist Service Assessment
section.text: Date of Entry: 01/01/2004

section.title: Presenting Problems
section.text: Tony was expressing grandiose ideas and was aggressive and invasive.

section.title: Outcome of Specialist Service Assessment
section.text: Tony's symptoms indicate hypomania. Difficulties with accommodation were also identified.

section.title: Further Details or Concerns Relating to Current Contact Status
section.text: Contact at home address.

section.title: Care Teams or Organisations Involved in Care Delivery
Main Care Team
section.text:
Status: Organisation with MHA Section 117 responsibility.
Date when the first entry for this team was recorded: 04/05/2004

section.title: Involvement in Patient Care or Support
section.text: Uptown Social Services

section.title: Identifiers Used In Other Organisations
section.text: National Insurance No = AA 01 01 01A

section.title: Persons with a Connection to the Care of Patient

tr.td: Care Coordinator/Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker at Uptown Social Services

section.title: Diagnosis
section.text:
Hypomania diagnosed on 12/2/2004.
ICD-10: F30

section.title: Registered Disability / Other Condition(s)
section.text: Tony has a hearing problem for which he has seen an Audiologist and has been fitted with appropriate new hearing aids.

section.title: Care Treatment Support Required
section.text: Tony has seen Audiologist

section.title: Problem Substances Information
section.text:
Primary Substance: Alcohol.
Age of first use = 12.
Taken Orally

section.title: Mental Health Act History
section.text: He was detained under MHA Section 2 on 01/01/2004, with symptoms of Hypomania. This was converted to a section 3 on 26/01/2004. Tony was removed from Section on 09/09/04.

section.title: Care Plan Summary
section.text: CPA has ended and there are no continuing care plan components at this time.

section.title: Contingency Plan
section.text: If Tony deteriorates the Uptown CMHT should be contacted to see if further care is needed. This will have to be treated as a new care spell and an MHA Assessment will need doing - if not done already.

section.title: Risk Assessment
section.text:
Date of Risk Assessment: 01/01/2004.
Risk Assessment Tool: Core Face

section.title: Risk Assessment Categories and Scale Scores
section.text:
Category: Violence to Adults.
Scale: MEDIUM.
Tony becomes verbally abusive when he doesn't get his way. Although he has not actually been physically violent he can be extremely intimidating.

section.title: HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment
section.text: HCR20 Assessment Date: 01/01/2004

section.title: HCR20 Outcome Score
section.text:
Category: H1 Previous Violence
Scale: 1
Indications have recently come to our attention that in his younger life Tony was convicted of Breach of the Peace connected to criminal activities - however there has not been any recent evidence of violent behaviours

section.title: Factors Increasing or Decreasing Risk
section.text:
Risk Factor: When he has had a considerable amount of alcohol he can be verbally aggressive.
Impact: Increases Risk

section.title: Risk Activities History
section.text:
Date: 01/05/2002.
Category: Aggression to Adults.
Level: High.
Tony has a long history of aggressive behaviour which whilst it has not seemed to extend into physical violence. He was arrested for a breach of the peace in May 2002 and was bound over to keep the peace at that time

section.title: Outcome / Measure / Assessment Information
section.text:
Tool: HoNOS.
Assessment Date 10/09/2006

section.title: HoNOS Tool Outcome Scores
section.text: Item 3: Alcohol/Drug Problem = 3 - moderately severe problem

section.title: Additional Information
section.text: No formal mental health history previously This care episode commenced on 01/01/04, with Tony admitted with indications of Hypomania. After an 8 month admission he was discharged to live independently in the community with support from the CMHT. He has good insight and is willing to engage with professionals.
